by_judge.judge: CHIEF JUSTICE & K.S. HEMALEKHA & VIJAYKUMAR A. PATIL
C M POONACHA WILL SIT IN COURT HALL NO.6

narrative:
On June 12, 2026, the Karnataka High Court recorded an overall hearing rate of 40.1%, resolving 1,099 of the 2,741 scheduled cases across 66 judges. This clearance efficiency marks a decline from the 47.5% hearing rate (1,275 of 2,687 cases) achieved on June 9, and sits slightly below the 41.4% benchmark registered on June 5. 

Dossier stages were led by "Orders," which comprised 1,044 listings and converted at 43.2% (451 cases heard). "Admission" matters showed stronger momentum, converting 57.0% of 335 scheduled cases, whereas "Further Hearing" stages lagged behind with a conversion rate of just 12.1% (32 heard of 264). Case type distribution was dominated by Writ Petitions (WP), which accounted for 1,248 scheduled cases but achieved only a 30.8% clearance rate (384 heard)—a drop from the 40.5% WP clearance rate seen on June 9. Meanwhile, Miscellaneous First Appeals (MFA) converted at 47.6%, and Regular Second Appeals (RSA) saw high clearance, resolving 69.0% of its 145 scheduled matters.

Individual judge listings saw varied throughput. In Court Hall 21, Judge V. Srishananda managed a highly productive docket, hearing 89 of 115 scheduled cases (77.4%). Judge E.S. Indiresh in Court Hall 16 also achieved notable progress, clearing 47 of 56 scheduled cases (83.9%). Conversely, massive block scheduling restricted performance on other dockets; the division bench of S.G. Pandit and Pradeep Singh Yerur cleared only 1 of 100 scheduled cases, while Judge B.M. Shyam Prasad left a single bulk listing of 314 scheduled cases entirely unheard, though he cleared several smaller, separate blocks in Court Hall 6.
